description_long: This object consists of two irregularly shaped, medium-sized stones fused together, with a rough, uneven surface texture. The stones display a mottled gray coloration with patches of green, suggesting the presence of moss or lichen, which gives them a natural, weathered appearance. The larger stone is more rounded and bulbous, while the smaller one is flatter and slightly angular, creating a visually interesting asymmetry. The surface is coarse and pitted, with subtle undulations and crevices that catch the light differently across its form. The coloration is predominantly gray, but the greenish tint is distributed unevenly, enhancing the sense of age and exposure to the elements. This type of stone is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of landscaping features along sidewalks and plazas.
description: A pair of irregular, moss-tinted gray stones with rough, weathered surfaces and natural asymmetry. The stones are fused together, featuring mottled textures and subtle green patches.
description_view_0: The front view shows the two stones stacked, with the larger stone on top and the smaller, flatter stone below, highlighting the uneven join and surface texture.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the rounded bulk of the larger stone and the flatter, more angular profile of the smaller stone, with visible greenish mottling.
description_view_2: From this angle, the object appears as a single, elongated stone, with the rough surface and green patches more uniformly distributed.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals the broader, rounded back of the larger stone and the squat, compact form of the smaller stone, with pronounced surface roughness.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling with patches of green moss and subtle pitting; no artificial carvings or regular veining.
attribute_weathering: Rough with visible moss and lichen, indicating prolonged exposure to moisture and natural elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with planted areas and other stones, partially covered by moss and possibly surrounded by grass or soil.
